title: Mythos, the artificial intelligence that discovered a memory leak and was blocked by Trump
summary: The Mythos model has been in the news for detecting the 'Squidbleed' vulnerability, for Trump's order to block its access to classified systems, and for passing cybersecurity tests against other models.

title: China presents the most powerful supercomputer in the world, surpassing the United States
summary: The new Chinese supercomputer tops the list of the fastest on the planet, marking a milestone in the technological race.

title: Valve expands SteamOS to Intel, AMD and Nvidia hardware
summary: Valve collaborates with major chip manufacturers so that SteamOS works on any PC, not just AMD machines.

title: Valve justifies the $1050 price of the Steam Machine
summary: The company states that the high cost is necessary to maintain the open PC ecosystem and does not subsidize the device.

title: Meta develops its own prediction markets application
summary: Mark Zuckerberg has ordered the creation of an event betting app, similar to Polymarket, for Meta.

title: Meta launches affordable smart glasses without Ray-Ban collaboration
summary: The new Meta Glasses do without the Ray-Ban brand to reduce costs and reach more users.

title: FUTO Swipe, a new swipe keyboard for mobile devices
summary: The gesture typing app promises a faster and more accurate experience than traditional keyboards.

title: LastPass suffers data leak due to attack on Klue platform
summary: LastPass support data and customer credentials were exposed due to a breach in Klue's supply chain.

title: Prime Day: the best deals on Amazon technology
summary: Smartwatches, peripherals, televisions and mini-PCs are some of the discounted products during Prime Day.

title: Europe faces extreme heat wave with record temperatures
summary: The heat wave causes melted roads in France and experts recommend changing cooling strategies in homes.

title: Oracle lays off 21,000 employees and bets on artificial intelligence
summary: The software company replaces much of its human workforce with AI technology in a massive restructuring.

title: Trump orders migration to post-quantum cryptography before 2030
summary: An executive order requires federal agencies to update their cryptographic systems to defend against future quantum attacks.

title: Five Eyes warns of imminent AI attacks in cybersecurity
summary: The intelligence alliance warns that AI-based attacks could occur within months and asks for quick patches.

title: SpaceX raises $25 billion in debt and its shares fall below the launch price
summary: The space company faces financial turmoil: it issues $25 billion in debt and its securities fall below $150.

title: Meta pauses tracking of employees' keystrokes to train AI
summary: The company stops collecting typing data after an internal leak revealed the practice.

title: 47% of Harvard students admit to having cheated
summary: A survey reveals a high rate of academic dishonesty at the university, a problem that existed before ChatGPT.

title: 60% of content on TikTok is generated by low-quality artificial intelligence
summary: A study indicates that most videos on TikTok are 'AI junk', while on YouTube they represent 21%.

title: Polymarket paid influencers to fake winning bets and promote itself
summary: The betting platform created fake websites and paid influencers to simulate victories and attract users.

title: Wikipedia co-founder Larry Sanger expelled for political campaigning
summary: Sanger was blocked from the site for trying to create a conservative pressure group within the encyclopedia.

title: Madison Square Garden compiled a dossier on activists critical of facial recognition
summary: The document collected comments and tweets from opponents of the stadium's biometric identification system.
